Welcome, Danilo. So, tell me, what does a typical day look like for you?
Thanks, Sofia. I usually start with our team standup at nine, and then I check the pipelines.
Nice. And how do you manage the workload across the team?
I prioritize the urgent tasks first, and I often delegate the smaller ones to my engineers.
Good. Who do you report to on the data side?
I report to you now. My team tracks every deadline, and we catch up every Monday.
Perfect. And what about nights and weekends?
One engineer is always on call, so we can handle any urgent problem quickly.
Thats a solid routine. I think youll do great here.
Thank you, Sofia. Im looking forward to it.
I usually start my day with a team standup.
I prioritize the urgent tasks and delegate the rest.
My team handles the pipelines and tracks every deadline.
I report to the director, and we catch up every Monday.
One engineer is always on call at night.

Hi Danilo, it's Sofia. Welcome to the leadership team. Let me give you a quick picture of how we work. The data team usually starts the day with a short standup at nine o'clock. During the week, the engineers handle the pipelines and track every deadline closely. We rarely deploy on Fridays, because we always keep that day for reviews and planning. As the lead, you report to me, and we catch up every Monday morning. When something urgent happens at night, one engineer is on call. It is a busy team, but a strong one. I am sure you will fit right in. Talk soon.
